title: Intro to Building Robots: Hands-on Workshop (XRP Platform)

venue.name: Zelifcam
venue.address: 10200 San Pedro Ave, San Antonio, TX, 78216

description:
Ever wanted to build and program your own robot but didn't know where to start? Skip the dry lectures and join us at **Alamo Tech Collective's Hackerspace** for a high-energy, fully hands-on workshop where you will build, program, and deploy a wheeled autonomous robot from scratch!
We are using the brand-new **SparkFun Experiential Robotics Platform (XRP)**, a tool-free, beginner-friendly robotics kit designed to make learning robotics fast, intuitive, and incredibly fun.
Whether you are a software developer looking to bridge into hardware, a student curious about automation, or an absolute beginner who has never touched code, this workshop is designed for you.

**🛠️ What We'll Do:**
We will pair up in teams of two (one "Driver" managing assembly/hardware and one "Programmer" handling the code, switching roles throughout) to tackle real-world engineering challenges:

* **Part 1: The Build (Tool-Free Assembly)** – Piece together your robot's chassis, mount the motors, and plug in the controller.
* **Part 2: Hello World, Robot Style** – Write your first program to make your robot drive, spin, and perform a custom "robot dance."
* **Part 3: The Precision Driving Challenge** – Program your robot to navigate a taped obstacle course utilizing differential steering.
* **Part 4: Robot Perception (Sensors)** – Integrate an ultrasonic distance sensor to teach your robot how to see, back up, and avoid obstacles dynamically.
* **Part 5: Autonomous Grand Prix** – Put it all together! Compete in a final tournament featuring line-following tracks and autonomous navigation challenges.

👥 **Who This is For:**

* Ages 16 to adult.
* Absolute beginners! No prior robotics, electronics, or programming experience required.
* Developers wanting hands-on experience with hardware/embedded logic.

💻 **What to Bring:**

* **A laptop or Chromebook** with a USB port (or appropriate adapter) and a charger.
* An open mind and a collaborative attitude!

**🎙️ About your Instructors:**
This session is led by **Daniel Espinoza** and **Microchips & Queso Robotics**, local robotics educators dedicated to making hardware engineering accessible to the San Antonio community.

**Robot kits are instructional aids only and may not be taken.**

title: Build Your Own AI Agent with Hermes
description:
**You keep hearing "just use an AI agent," so let's actually build one. Together. On your own laptop.**
Hermes Agent is an open-source (MIT) agent from Nous Research that lives on your machine, remembers what it learns, and writes its own reusable skills as it goes. No SaaS lock-in, no telemetry, no "trust us with your data." Just an agent you own.
In this hands-on workshop, Brandon Howard walks the room through getting Hermes installed and running, then we build one real automation together — start to finish. You leave with a working agent on your laptop, not a pile of notes you'll never open again.
**What We're Building (Together)**

* Install Hermes locally (macOS, Windows, or Linux) and get past first-run setup
* Wire up a model provider the easy way — one free OAuth login, zero API-key juggling
* Run your first agent conversation and watch it use real tools
* Build one practical automation end-to-end (think: a scheduled briefing or a repo summarizer)
* See how Hermes remembers context and writes its own skill, so it never resolves the same problem

**What You'll Walk Away With**

* A working AI agent is installed and running on your own laptop
* One automation you actually built, ready to keep using
* A mental model for what "agentic" really means — beyond the hype
* The confidence to keep experimenting on your own

**Who Should Attend**
Curious developers of every stripe. If you can open a terminal and type a command when someone shows you, you're ready. No prior agent experience required — we build from the ground up, and there's help in the room if you get stuck.
**What to Bring**

* A laptop (macOS, Windows, or Linux) with admin rights to install software
* A charger, we'll be installing and running things for a couple of hours
* Optional but recommended: a free Nous Portal account created ahead of time

**Format**
\~2-hour guided build-along. Install together, build together, ask questions out loud. Informal, hands-on, zero pitches.
